git_commit: a57e731 (last manual commit, unchanged since 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z — now 171.8h / 7.16d old, 7-day threshold crossed). 1fd2aef (2026-04-24T07:12:42Z) ECM Daily Review auto-regen this window, no registry movement. Current HEAD: 6d80712 (2026-04-24T15:39:47Z, auto).
notes: Two milestones crossed this poll. (a) The 7-DAY manual-commit drought threshold has been crossed — last manual commit now 7.16 days old. The prior two consecutive polls flagged 6-day imminent; it is now past. (b) Silent-ignore is now pipeline-canonical across THREE consecutive daily_review auto-regens (07:06Z Apr 22, 07:09Z Apr 23, 07:12Z Apr 24). Three auto-regens holding the line takes the 'algorithmic policy' framing from 'two consecutive = possibly coincidence' to 'clearly the steady-state behaviour of build_daily_review.py'. Counterargument for record (per user preference): a 7-day manual-commit gap over a weekend-plus-bridge is not inherently anomalous for a personal project, and the author has shipped large manual pushes before (e.g. 2026-04-17 shipped 3 manual commits in ~25 min). The drought is only suspicious when read alongside PRED-105 silent-ignore — in isolation it is merely 'author on break'. Opposing considerations: (1) during comparable 'author on break' periods earlier in the repo history the auto-pipeline's registry_ratio_pct tracked actual passes/failures; now it holds constant through what would otherwise be an acknowledged-failure event, which is a behaviour change in the automation not just in author activity; (2) the build_daily_review.py logic has been unchanged since a57e731 (2026-04-17) — the script is deterministically producing the same result because PRED-105's state file has not been touched, not because the script can't handle window expiry; (3) the site continues to accept new auto snapshots (392 snapshots in status_history.json, latest 2026-04-24T15:39:43Z) so the pipeline is live and healthy — silent-ignore is what the live system is choosing to emit; (4) PRED-105 cucumber-growth test is the cleanest measurable in the April window — rodent vs. lab assay vs. field measurement ambiguity does not apply. Next-poll escalation watch: (1) goalpost-move on PRED-105 window text -> CRITICAL, (2) new WIN-070 with PRED-105-related claim -> CRITICAL per Step 5 hard-escalation, (3) any manual commit breaks ~172h+ streak (well past 7-day threshold) -> substantive minimum with canary/parameter diff required — large batch commits after extended silence are themselves notable, (4) refined count moving from 4 to 5 in next daily_review auto-regen (~2026-04-25T07:15Z) would indicate post-hoc adjudication absorbing PRED-105, (5) PRED-106 'April 2026' window edges closer to closure (ambiguous; monitor for silent-ignore there too). Analyst handoff: the silent-ignore PIPELINE-CANONICAL dimension is now 3 consecutive auto-regens deep — if an uncounted-failures expansion to note algorithmic-policy dimension is in flight, worth refreshing the count.
